title: ldircreate
text: ldircreate Create the specified directory in the working directory, ignoring redirections in the assignment file. ldircreate NewDirPath Example: ldircreate "exports" Parameters Table 1. Parameters Parameter Data Type Description NewDirPath String Directory name, optionally preceded by the path to the directory, which can be in a zip archive. Must end with a directory separator. Return Values Table 2. Values returned by $procerror Value Error constant Meaning 0 Successful -13 <UIOSERR_OS_COMMAND> An error occurred while trying to perform the OS command. Set /pri=64 to display the exact error in the message frame. Use Allowed in all component types. Description The ldircreate statement creates the specified directory in the current working directory, ignoring any file redirections in the assignment file. In other respects, it works the same as dircreate . For more information, see dircreate . Related Links ProcScript for File System Management Uniface provides ProcScript statements and functions for managing local files and directories. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
